active.title: Authorize LDO Accumulation Program for up to 10,000 stETH
active.body:
## TL;DR

The proposal seeks to authorize the [Lido Growth Committee](https://research.lido.fi/t/rationalizing-liquidity-observation-lab-rewards-share-committee-growth-committee/10133) to execute a one-time LDO accumulation program using up to 10,000 stETH from the Lido DAO Treasury.

Execution will be facilitated by the Lido Ecosystem Foundation and carried out by the Growth Committee, with discretion over execution timing and venues within predefined constraints, provided current market condit
active.author: 0x8Ee5143fb47d3234B36d6a77dEb88fbd8047Fbc1
active.impact: High-impact proposal affecting LDO directly — review required
active.ai_summary: The proposal authorizes the Lido Growth Committee to execute a one-time LDO token accumulation program using up to 10,000 stETH from the Lido DAO Treasury. If it passes, the Growth Committee gains discretion over execution timing and venues within predefined constraints to buy back LDO tokens.
active.ai_impact_reason: Treasury deployment for token buyback affects wstETH holder value

active.title: From LNOSG to CMC: Evolving Curated Staking Modules Governance
active.body:
## TL;DR

The proposal introduces the Curated Module Committee (CMC) as a dedicated body to handle the day-to-day operations of Lido’s Curated Modules in light of the upcoming [Curated Module v2](https://research.lido.fi/t/future-of-the-curated-module-cmv2-landscape/10929) (CMv2). The CMC is proposed to be granted governance authority over CMv2, oversight of CMv1 and Simple DVT Module (SDVTM), and the responsibilities currently performed by LNOSG (Lido Node Operator Sub-Governance Group), which
active.author: 0xFf64362EBf794a22A23E12666C4f875A31581fCe
active.impact: Medium impact — operational change for LDO
active.ai_summary: The proposal introduces the Curated Module Committee (CMC) to handle day-to-day operations of Lido's Curated Modules in preparation for Curated Module v2 (CMv2). If it passes, the CMC receives governance authority over CMv2, oversight of CMv1 and Simple DVT Module, and assumes responsibilities currently performed by LNOSG.
active.ai_impact_reason: Governance restructuring with no direct fee or collateral impact

active.title: Redirect DVT & APM Incentives to Current Meta Treasury and LoL
active.body:
## Proposal Summary

This proposal aims to formalize the change in destination and utilization of post-Node Operator share of DVT incentives and APM incentives flows, and seeks DAO approval to:

- Redirect post-Node Operator share of DVT incentives flow arising from Obol and SSV Network providers (allocated under the recently [approved terms](https://snapshot.box/#/s:lido-snapshot.eth/proposal/0x8b8619f09213b868708b25cb2512023234e5e160cba5c291f4e9d944d2ccd3fa)) from the Decentralized Validator V
active.author: 0xFf64362EBf794a22A23E12666C4f875A31581fCe
active.impact: High-impact proposal affecting LDO directly — review required
active.ai_summary: The proposal redirects post-Node Operator share of DVT incentives and APM incentives flows from their current destination to the Meta Treasury and LoL (Liquidity observation Lab). If it passes, incentive flows arising from Obol and SSV Network providers will be redirected to these new destinations.
active.ai_impact_reason: Incentive flow redirection, no direct token-holder value change

active.title: Introduce an Identified DVT Cluster type in the Community Staking Module
active.body:
## TL;DR

This proposal introduces a new CSM Node Operator type — Identified DVT Cluster (IDVTC) — allowing groups of 4 Identified Community Stakers (ICS) to run validators collaboratively using DVT. The design combines the trust guarantees of ICS with the resilience of DVT, offering improved capital efficiency while maintaining strict eligibility requirements and DAO oversight. For full proposal details, please refer to the [Research Forum](https://research.lido.fi/t/community-staking-module/59
active.author: 0xFf64362EBf794a22A23E12666C4f875A31581fCe
active.ai_summary: The proposal introduces a new CSM Node Operator type called Identified DVT Cluster (IDVTC), allowing groups of 4 Identified Community Stakers to run validators collaboratively using DVT. If it passes, the Community Staking Module will support this new operator type combining ICS trust guarantees with DVT resilience.
active.ai_impact_reason: Operational expansion of staking module, no fee impact

active.title: Lido DAO Ops Multisigs Policy 3.0
active.body:
## Motivation

Operating DAO-related processes requires striking a balance between flexibility and security. Lido DAO and the Lido protocol are governed by tokenholder voting, while Safe multisig wallets are used to execute a broad set of delegated functions, helping streamline numerous operational processes while remaining transparent and subject to DAO oversight and potential veto.

This proposal:

- Introduces a new Lido DAO Ops Multisigs Policy 3.0, effectively replacing [the previous versio
active.author: 0xFf64362EBf794a22A23E12666C4f875A31581fCe
active.impact: High-impact proposal affecting LDO directly — review required
active.ai_summary: The proposal introduces a new Lido DAO Ops Multisigs Policy 3.0, replacing the previous version to update governance processes for Safe multisig wallets executing delegated DAO functions. If it passes, the new policy framework governs how operational multisigs operate under DAO oversight.
active.ai_impact_reason: Administrative policy update for multisig governance

recent_closed.title: Integrate Lido into the Galaxy Challenge Game 🚀
recent_closed.body:
Galaxy Challenge is a skill-based Web3 arcade game where players pilot a starship, dodge crypto coins, and survive waves of increasing difficulty. We propose integrating Lido (stETH/wstETH) as a supported deposit & reward token.

Also, The idea is that players will also see the Lido logo in the game, helping to promote Lido in a fun and engaging way.

Galaxy Challange: [https://Galaxy.gift](url)

The Lido logo will appear in the game, and the spaceship can shoot at the logo as part of the gamepl

recent_closed.title: [RFC] Governance Process Upgrade: Establishing a Statute of Limitations for Temperature Checks
recent_closed.body:
Author: @Eren_Targ5 (Daoplomats)

## Summary
This proposal seeks to establish a formal "Statute of Limitations" for Uniswap Governance proposals. Specifically, it mandates that any proposal which passes a "Temperature Check" (Snapshot vote) must proceed to an on-chain vote within 90 days.

If a proposal fails to move to an on-chain vote within this 90-day window, the Temperature Check is declared "Expired" (or "Dead"). To proceed, the proposer must restart the governance process with a new Tempe
recent_closed.author: 0x3FB19771947072629C8EEE7995a2eF23B72d4C8A

_This is the first proposal to use the new governance process_ [_approved_](https://gov.uniswap.org/t/unification-proposal/25881#p-57882-protocol-fee-rollout-4) _in UNIfication. The new process only applies to fee parameter updates, where proposals can bypass the RFC stage and go directly to a five-day Snapshot followed by an onchain vote. This allows for faster updates to protocol fees, while retaining the

recent_closed.title: [6.31] [Temp Check] Delegation Incentives Program
recent_closed.body:
## Abstract
ENS is safer when voting power sits with active delegates, not idle wallets. We propose a 90-day pilot that pays incentives to both active delegates and their delegators. Guardrails: (1) a balance-per-second factor for delegators (capped at 180 days) to reduce sybils, (2) a 1 ENS minimum payout per address to avoid inefficient micro-transfers, and (3) payout caps per-delegate and per-delegator to avoid concentration and strengthen long-term capture cost.

## Specification
### Context

recent_closed.title: [6.26] [Social] ENS Retro: An ENS DAO Retrospective & Stakeholder Analysis
recent_closed.body:
# Summary
DAOs have had almost a decade of history, during which there have been very few retrospective reviews of DAO treasury spend. This proposal aims to bring more accountability to the DAO’s spending, developing clear and measurable datapoints that the DAO can use to continue to build its processes and decision making mechanisms.

This temp check proposes undertaking a formalised retrospective on ENS DAO; its impact, its spending, its goals and its outputs from the past two years. Examining
